# Saving KC Homebuyers — Full Context

> We buy houses for cash in the Kansas City metro area. No repairs, no agents, no fees. Close in as few as 7 days.

## Company Information

- Name: Saving KC Homebuyers
- Short Name: Saving KC
- Owner: Ernest Dodson
- Phone: (816) 429-2900
- Email: ari@savingkc.com
- Address: 1705 Baltimore Ave, Kansas City, MO 64108
- Website: https://savingkc.com
- Founded: 2020
- Google Rating: 4.9 stars
- Business Type: Cash home buyer / real estate investor

## What We Do

Saving KC Homebuyers is a cash home buying company serving the Kansas City metropolitan area across Missouri and Kansas. We purchase single-family homes, duplexes, triplexes, and small multi-family properties (up to 4 units) in any condition — foundation cracks, fire damage, mold, water damage, hoarder conditions, code violations, condemned properties, and more.

Homeowners sell to us when the traditional listing process doesn't work for their situation:
- They need to sell fast (foreclosure, divorce, relocation)
- They can't afford repairs ($5,000–$80,000+ in renovation costs)
- They inherited a property and don't want to deal with probate, cleanout, or repairs
- They have tax liens, back taxes, or judgment liens on the property
- They have problem tenants or vacant properties draining money

### Our Process

1. **Contact Us** — Call (816) 429-2900 or fill out the online form. Tell us about the property, its condition, and your timeline. Takes about 5 minutes. No obligation.
2. **Get Your Cash Offer** — We visit the property and present a written cash offer within 24 hours. We show exactly how we calculated the number.
3. **Choose Your Closing Date** — Accept the offer and pick a closing date: as soon as 7 days or up to 60 days.
4. **Get Paid** — Sign at a local title company and receive funds via wire transfer or cashier's check the same day.

### What Sets Us Apart

- We pay ALL closing costs (title fees, recording fees, transfer taxes)
- No agent commissions (0% vs. 5–6% with traditional listing)
- No repairs needed — we buy completely as-is
- No cleaning or cleanout — leave anything you don't want
- Cash purchase — no financing contingencies, no appraisal delays
- We handle tax liens, probate coordination, and title issues
- 11+ years of experience in the KC metro

---

## Service Area

5 counties, 35 cities, 100+ neighborhoods across the Kansas City metro.

### Missouri Counties

#### Jackson County, MO
- Population: 717,204
- Median Home Value: $195,000
- County Seat: Kansas City
- Courthouse: Jackson County Courthouse, 415 E 12th St, Kansas City, MO 64106
- Tax Deadline: December 31
- Tax Sale: August (tax lien certificate sale)
- Redemption: 1 year after lien sale
- Probate Court: Jackson County Circuit Court – Probate Division
- Page: https://savingkc.com/mo/jackson/

### Kansas Counties

#### Wyandotte County, KS
- Population: 165,429
- Median Home Value: $135,000
- County Seat: Kansas City, KS
- Courthouse: Wyandotte County Courthouse, 710 N 7th St, Kansas City, KS 66101
- Tax Deadline: December 20
- Tax Sale: Sheriff's sale (judicial tax foreclosure under K.S.A. 79-2801)
- Redemption: Day before sheriff's sale ONLY — no post-sale redemption in Kansas
- Page: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/

Cities:
- Kansas City, KS (9 neighborhoods): https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/
  - Strawberry Hill: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/strawberry-hill/
  - Argentine: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/argentine/
  - Armourdale: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/armourdale/
  - Rosedale: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/rosedale/
  - Quindaro: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/quindaro/
  - Riverview: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/riverview/
  - Turner / Muncie: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/turner-muncie/
  - Bethel / Piper: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/bethel-piper/
  - Village West / Legends: https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/kansas-city/village-west-legends/
- Bonner Springs (2 neighborhoods): https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/bonner-springs/
- Edwardsville (2 neighborhoods): https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/edwardsville/
- Lake Quivira (1 neighborhood): https://savingkc.com/ks/wyandotte/lake-quivira/

- Redemption: Day before sheriff's sale ONLY — no post-sale redemption in Kansas

---

## Legal Context

### Missouri Tax Sales (RSMo Chapter 140)
- Property tax due December 31
- 1% per month penalty on unpaid balance
- Tax lien certificate sold at public auction (typically August)
- Owner retains property after lien sale
- 1-year redemption period: must pay full lien + 10% interest + costs
- If not redeemed: lien holder petitions for collector's deed → ownership transfers permanently
- Reassessment cycle: every 2 years (odd years)

### Missouri Probate (RSMo Chapter 473)
- Independent administration: personal representative can sell property with minimal court oversight
- Supervised administration: requires court approval for each transaction
- Letters Testamentary: court document authorizing estate representative to sell property
- Small-estate affidavit: available if personal property ≤ $40,000
- Electronic wills recognized after August 28, 2025
- Typical timeline: 6–18 months
- Property can be sold during probate with appropriate authorization

### Kansas Tax Foreclosure (K.S.A. 79-2801)
- Property tax due December 20
- County attorney files judicial foreclosure in district court
- Sheriff's sale ordered by the court
- Redemption right expires the day BEFORE sheriff's sale
- NO post-sale redemption in Kansas
- Once sheriff's deed records, ownership transfers permanently
- Reassessment: annually

### Kansas Probate (K.S.A. Chapter 59)
- Unsupervised administration (most common): personal representative has broad authority
- Supervised administration: requires court approval per transaction
- Transfer-on-death (TOD) deeds: bypass probate entirely
- Summary administration: available if estate ≤ $40,000
- Typical timeline: 6–12 months (faster than Missouri)

---

## Situations We Handle (Detail)

Each situation has a dedicated page with Kansas City-specific guidance:

1. **Foundation Issues** — https://savingkc.com/situations/foundation-issues/
   Homes with cracked foundations, bowing walls, settling. Repair costs: $5,000–$80,000+.

2. **Fire Damage** — https://savingkc.com/situations/fire-damage/
   Partial or total fire damage. Insurance complications. We buy regardless of damage severity.

3. **Water Damage** — https://savingkc.com/situations/water-damage/
   Flooding, pipe bursts, basement water intrusion. Mold risk. Repair costs: $3,000–$50,000+.

4. **Mold** — https://savingkc.com/situations/mold/
   Active mold, black mold, remediation needed. Buyers with financing can't close on moldy homes.

5. **Hoarder House** — https://savingkc.com/situations/hoarder-house/
   Extreme clutter, biohazard conditions. We handle complete cleanout after closing.

6. **Inherited Property** — https://savingkc.com/situations/inherited-property/
   Probate properties, out-of-state heirs, multiple owners. We coordinate with courts and attorneys.

7. **Divorce** — https://savingkc.com/situations/divorce/
   Shared property, court-ordered sales, splitting proceeds. Fast closings for clean breaks.

8. **Behind on Payments** — https://savingkc.com/situations/behind-on-payments/
   Late mortgage payments, forbearance ending. Sell before credit damage compounds.

9. **Pre-Foreclosure** — https://savingkc.com/situations/pre-foreclosure/
   Notice of default received. We close fast enough to stop foreclosure proceedings.

10. **Vacant Property** — https://savingkc.com/situations/vacant-property/
    Ongoing costs (taxes, insurance, maintenance) on a property nobody lives in.

11. **Bad Tenants** — https://savingkc.com/situations/bad-tenants/
    Problem tenants, eviction situations. We buy tenant-occupied properties.

12. **Code Violations** — https://savingkc.com/situations/code-violations/
    Open permits, condemned status, occupancy issues. We handle all code compliance after purchase.

---

## Guides & Resources

- **Probate Guide**: https://savingkc.com/probate/
  Complete guide to selling inherited property during or after probate in MO & KS.

- **Tax-Delinquent Properties**: https://savingkc.com/tax-delinquent/
  How tax liens, redemption periods, and foreclosure work in the KC metro.

- **Jackson County Tax Redemption**: https://savingkc.com/tax-delinquent/redemption/jackson-county/
  Redemption rights, deadlines, and costs specific to Jackson County.

- **Jackson County Payment Plans**: https://savingkc.com/tax-delinquent/payment-plans/jackson-county/
  Tax payment plan options and eligibility for Jackson County property owners.

- **Johnson County Payment Plans**: https://savingkc.com/tax-delinquent/payment-plans/johnson-county/
  Tax payment plan options and eligibility for Johnson County property owners.
